name: instantiation
description: Instantiation is only enabled for universal (`\forall`) or existential (`\exists`) predicates. For instantiations of existential predicates, you must use existing constants defined in the context. e.g. To instantiate `\exists x \cdot x \in TYPE`, you need to inspect the constants and axioms, and make sure to choose a ***constant from the context*** with the correct type `TYPE`, not variables from the machine. For example, axioms `t \in TYPE` and `partition(TYPE, {t1, t2})` indicate that t, t1, t2 are in TYPE set. You instantiation could be either t, t1, or t2. If `TYPE` is NAT or INT, you can choose 1,2,3,... as instantiation.

node_id.description: ID of the node in the proof tree to apply this function on.
